title: Update Theme Colors to New Brand Palette
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the app to use the updated brand color palette, so that the interface has a professional and consistent color scheme where green indicates interactive elements.
description:
Update the application color scheme to the new brand palette. Green (`#08BC8C`) should primarily indicate clickable/interactive elements rather than serving as a background color.

## Color Changes
- Update `.dtp-green` class to use `#08BC8C`
- Update disabled button style: background `#b0c8c1`, text color `#222222`
- Update toggle control colors in local `bootstrap.min.css`:
  - `.custom-control-input:checked ~ .custom-control-label::before` border and background: `#08BC8C`
  - Toggle text color: `#222222`
- Remove `min-height-200` class from the header background area
acceptance_criteria: dtp-green class uses #08BC8C as the primary green
Disabled buttons show #b0c8c1 background with #222222 text
Header area no longer uses min-height-200 constraint
Green is reserved for interactive/clickable elements across the UI
ux_changes: Comprehensive color scheme update across the application. Primary green changed to #08BC8C for interactive elements. Disabled button and toggle control styles updated. Header background area height constraint removed.
implementation_notes: Updated both the custom CSS (.dtp-green class) and the local bootstrap.min.css for toggle controls. Developer raised a valid concern about modifying bootstrap.min.css directly (upgrade risk), but the change was applied to the local copy. Caching issues required incognito testing to verify changes.
comment_summary: Multiple rounds of color adjustments. Developer updated .dtp-green but raised concerns about modifying bootstrap.min.css (upgrade compatibility). Ryan identified the specific toggle CSS rule to update. Caching required incognito testing to verify changes.
